trade.summary: Significant bilateral trade. Svizzera exports pharmaceuticals, machinery, watches, and luxury goods. Arabia Saudita exports petroleum products to European markets. Svizzera imports oil through European networks. Trade relations are driven largely by energy and luxury goods.

diplomatic.summary: Pragmatic bilateral relations. Svizzera maintains official relations with Arabia Saudita while also engaging with Gulf states through its neutral diplomacy. Geneva hosts Saudi-related international talks and Red Cross operations. Human rights concerns (Khashoggi case, Yemen war) create diplomatic friction. Svizzera balances humanitarian principles with economic interests.
recent_incidents: Swiss human rights concerns over Arabia Saudita's Yemen military campaign

military.summary: Svizzera maintains strict arms export controls and has generally refused to sell weapons to Arabia Saudita given the Yemen conflict. No military alliance or joint exercises. Some Swiss defense technology firms have Gulf connections.
incidents: Swiss parliament voted to restrict arms exports to Saudi Arabia (Yemen conflict)

financial.summary: Svizzera manages significant Saudi private wealth and sovereign wealth (through PIF-related investments). Swiss banks are key conduits for Saudi capital into European markets. Major Swiss financial institutions have Arabia Saudita offices. Bilateral investment is substantial.

energy.summary: Arabia Saudita is an indirect energy supplier to Svizzera through European crude oil markets. Svizzera imports refined petroleum products derived partly from Saudi crude. Some cooperation on energy transition and clean energy investments.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific cooperation. KAStati UnitiT-Swiss university partnerships in materials science and clean energy. Saudi students study at Swiss universities. Joint publications growing from a low base.
